note: oEmbed Providers extends core Media with two config entities (custom provider + provider bucket), a ProviderRepository decorator, a media source override, one permission, and an alter hook. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with core Media, I need to embed content from a provider that core does not list (it isn't in oembed.com/providers.json). How do I add a custom oEmbed provider so core Media will use it? Name the module and the config entity.
evals.expected_output: Install the oEmbed Providers module and add a custom provider (the oembed_provider config entity) at /admin/config/media/oembed-providers/custom-providers, giving a provider name, provider URL and endpoint(s). It is merged into Media's provider list.

evals.prompt: Which two config entity types does the oEmbed Providers module define, and what are their config prefixes?
evals.expected_output: oembed_provider (config prefix 'provider', i.e. oembed_providers.provider.*) and oembed_provider_bucket (config prefix 'bucket', i.e. oembed_providers.bucket.*).

evals.prompt: I want a Media type that only accepts a specific set of oEmbed providers. Using oEmbed Providers, how do I restrict the allowed providers, and what is the resulting media source machine name for a bucket whose machine name is 'remote_image'?
evals.expected_output: Create a provider bucket selecting the allowed providers; it is exposed as a media source named oembed:{bucket_id} (here oembed:remote_image). Assign that source to the Media type to restrict its providers.

evals.prompt: With oEmbed Providers, how can I override core Media's default 'Remote video' (oembed:video) media source so it uses my own chosen providers?
evals.expected_output: Create a provider bucket with the machine name 'video' — it produces the oembed:video source and overrides core's default Remote video source.

evals.prompt: How does the oEmbed Providers module get its custom providers into the list that core Media actually uses? Name the mechanism/service.
evals.expected_output: It decorates the core media.oembed.provider_repository service with ProviderRepositoryDecorator, whose getAll() merges custom providers into the fetched provider list.

evals.prompt: I want a Drupal site to use ONLY my locally defined oEmbed providers and not fetch the remote oembed.com providers list. Which setting controls this and in which config object?
evals.expected_output: Set external_fetch to false on the oembed_providers.settings config object (via the settings form or drush cset); then only locally-defined custom providers are used.

evals.prompt: Which permission gates managing custom oEmbed providers, provider buckets, and the settings form in the oEmbed Providers module?
evals.expected_output: The single 'administer oembed providers' permission gates all of it (settings form plus both config entities).

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to add or modify oEmbed providers programmatically, in the list oEmbed Providers builds. Which hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_oembed_providers_alter(array &$providers) — it fires in ProviderRepositoryDecorator::getAll() before Provider objects are built.
